text: The mountains are calling and I must go.
author: John Muir

text: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.
author: John Muir

text: The clearest way into the Universe is through a forest wilderness.
author: John Muir

text: Of all the paths you take in life, make sure a few of them are dirt.
author: John Muir

text: Between every two pines there is a doorway to a new world.
author: John Muir

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Live in the sunshine, swim the sea, drink the wild air.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: In the wilderness I find something more dear and connate than in streets or villages.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The Earth has music for those who listen.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Walking is a man's best medicine.
author: Hippocrates

text: I took a walk in the woods and came out taller than the trees.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: An early morning walk is a blessing for the whole day.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: We do not see nature with our eyes, but with our understandings and our hearts.
author: William Hazlitt

text: The world is big and I want to have a good look at it before it gets dark.
author: John Muir

text: Go where you feel most alive.
author: Unknown

text: Climb the mountain so you can see the world, not so the world can see you.
author: David McCullough Jr.

text: It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves.
author: Sir Edmund Hillary

text: Because in the end, you won't remember the time you spent working in the office. Climb that mountain.
author: Jack Kerouac

text: The best view comes after the hardest climb.
author: Unknown

text: There are no shortcuts to any place worth going.
author: Beverly Sills

text: Every mountain top is within reach if you just keep climbing.
author: Barry Finlay

text: Somewhere between the bottom of the climb and the summit is the answer to the mystery why we climb.
author: Greg Child

text: Getting to the top is optional. Getting down is mandatory.
author: Ed Viesturs

text: May your trails be crooked, winding, lonesome, dangerous, leading to the most amazing view.
author: Edward Abbey

text: Wilderness is not a luxury but a necessity of the human spirit.
author: Edward Abbey

text: One touch of nature makes the whole world kin.
author: William Shakespeare

text: In the spring, at the end of the day, you should smell like dirt.
author: Margaret Atwood

text: Leave the road, take the trails.
author: Pythagoras

text: I go to nature to be soothed and healed, and to have my senses put in order.
author: John Burroughs

text: The trail is the thing, not the end of the trail.
author: Louis L'Amour

text: Thousands of tired, nerve-shaken, over-civilized people are beginning to find out that going to the mountains is going home.
author: John Muir

text: The wilderness holds answers to questions we have not yet learned to ask.
author: Nancy Wynne Newhall

text: Keep close to Nature's heart and break clear away once in a while to climb a mountain or spend a week in the woods.
author: John Muir

text: I felt my lungs inflate with the onrush of scenery — air, mountains, trees, people.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: A walk in nature walks the soul back home.
author: Mary Davis

text: And into the forest I go, to lose my mind and find my soul.
author: John Muir

text: To walk in nature is to witness a thousand miracles.
author: Mary Davis

text: Hiking is the answer. Who cares what the question is.
author: Unknown

text: On earth there is no heaven, but there are pieces of it.
author: Jules Renard

text: Two roads diverged in a wood, and I took the one less traveled by.
author: Robert Frost

text: Study nature, love nature, stay close to nature. It will never fail you.
author: Frank Lloyd Wright

text: The world reveals itself to those who travel on foot.
author: Werner Herzog

text: Returning home is the most difficult part of long-distance hiking. You have grown outside the puzzle and your piece no longer fits.
author: Cindy Ross

text: Life begins at the end of your comfort zone.
author: Neale Donald Walsch

text: Over every mountain there is a path, although it may not be seen from the valley.
author: Theodore Roethke

text: If the winter is too cold and the summer is too hot, you are not a hiker.
author: Unknown
